overview.mechanism: Primary ICU mechanism for norepinephrine targeting hemodynamic stability and critical care support.
indications: ICU use for vasopressors needs
Hemodynamic or emergent stabilization as indicated
Condition-specific protocol use per unit policy
contraindications: Use caution with severe end-organ compromise

dosing.adultDose: Start low-dose infusion and titrate to hemodynamic response per ICU protocol.
dosing.weightBased: Weight-based dosing calculator placeholder: [patientWeightKg × protocol factor].
dosing.bolusVsInfusion: Bolus for immediate effect when indicated; transition to infusion for sustained control.
dosing.titration: Titrate q2-3 min to MAP goal (typically ≥65 mmHg) or ordered SBP/HR target.
dosing.maxDose: Maximum dose per institutional policy and pharmacy verification.
dosing.target: MAP 65-75 mmHg; HR <120 bpm.

administration.ivRate: Administer via smart pump with guardrails and dual RN verification for high-risk doses.
administration.concentration: Use standardized concentration from pharmacy-prepared ICU admixtures.
administration.dilution: Dilute per institutional compatibility charts and manufacturer guidance.
administration.compatibility: Check Y-site compatibility before co-infusion; avoid known incompatible pairs.
administration.stability: Observe bag/syringe beyond-use dating, light protection, and tubing change intervals.
administration.line: Prefer central line for continuous vasoactive or irritant infusions; peripheral only per protocol.

before: Baseline HR, BP, MAP, RR, SpO2, pain/sedation status
Verify allergies, indication, and independent double-check when required
Confirm line patency and pump library selection
during: Continuous ECG and frequent BP/MAP trending
Trend urine output, lactate, and relevant labs
Assess for infiltration/extravasation and adverse effects
after: Reassess target response within protocol interval
Document dose changes, response, and safety checks
Handoff active titration plan and monitoring priorities
nursing.holdNotify: Hold for severe adverse effects or overshoot targets; notify MD/APP for instability, refractory symptoms, or protocol thresholds.
nursing.titrationTriggers: Escalate/de-escalate dose based on MAP/HR trend, perfusion markers, and protocol-defined triggers.

drugDrug: Potential interaction with other vasoactive or QT-prolonging agents
May potentiate hypotension/hypertension with concomitant cardioactive drugs
Review anticoagulant/antiplatelet overlap when applicable
ivIncompatibilities: Avoid same line with known precipitating agents
Separate line for incompatible pH-dependent infusions
Flush line before and after intermittent push medications
food: Enteral interactions may alter absorption for oral formulations
Follow tube-feed hold guidance when applicable

sideEffects.blackBox: Review FDA boxed warning where applicable before initiation.
sideEffects.antidote: Use category-specific reversal strategy and supportive care; consult pharmacy/toxicology for severe toxicity.

Verify allergies, indication, and independent double-check when required
Confirm line patency and pump library selection
during: Continuous ECG and frequent BP/MAP trending
Trend urine output, lactate, and relevant labs
Assess for infiltration/extravasation and adverse effects
after: Reassess target response within protocol interval
Document dose changes, response, and safety checks
Handoff active titration plan and monitoring priorities
nursing.holdNotify: Hold for severe adverse effects or overshoot targets; notify MD/APP for instability, refractory symptoms, or protocol thresholds.
nursing.titrationTriggers: Adjust based on symptom control, sedation/pain scales, and adverse effect profile.

drugDrug: Potential interaction with other vasoactive or QT-prolonging agents
May potentiate hypotension/hypertension with concomitant cardioactive drugs
Review anticoagulant/antiplatelet overlap when applicable
ivIncompatibilities: Avoid same line with known precipitating agents
Separate line for incompatible pH-dependent infusions
Flush line before and after intermittent push medications
food: Enteral interactions may alter absorption for oral formulations
Follow tube-feed hold guidance when applicable

sideEffects.blackBox: Review FDA boxed warning where applicable before initiation.
sideEffects.antidote: Use category-specific reversal strategy and supportive care; consult pharmacy/toxicology for severe toxicity.
